# techmeat.dev

> A working journal on the engineering practice of AI-assisted coding: how to brief agents, which specs and prompts hold up, and where the line sits between human judgment and agent execution. Authored by Sergey Eroshenkov.

The site is multilingual (English canonical, plus Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Russian, Arabic, Hindi, Chinese, and Bengali translations). All translated pages set `<link rel="canonical">` to the English version of the same article.

## Posts

- [All posts (English)](https://techmeat.dev/posts/): the full archive of long-form articles.
- [How I built this blog with coding agents](https://techmeat.dev/posts/building-techmeat-dev-with-coding-agents/): the story of launching techmeat.dev — from a spontaneous idea and a set of skills to Astro, Cloudflare Pages, brainstorming, and the first version of the blog.

## About the author

- [About](https://techmeat.dev/about/): who Sergey Eroshenkov is, what he works on, and what this blog tries to do.
- [Stack](https://techmeat.dev/stack/): tools, AI assistants, and the workflow used to ship posts and features with coding agents.

## Feeds

- [RSS — English (canonical)](https://techmeat.dev/rss.xml)
- [Privacy notice](https://techmeat.dev/privacy/)
